steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says that Chambers was associated with Sugar Hill Records, then contrasts that association with the fact that he did not play drums on the label's classic song "Rapper's Delight". The word however belongs in that first independent clause to mark the contrast.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation pattern
steps.content:
Choice B correctly places the comma inside the closing quotation mark and uses a semicolon after however: Delight," however;. The semicolon separates the next independent clause, he joined the label after the song's release.

Choice C is not wrong simply because it creates two sentences; it is wrong because it separates However from the clause it needs to interrupt. Choice A creates a comma splice, and choice D omits needed punctuation.

The answer is B.
